response:
**Results**

We evaluated our proposed method by conducting a comprehensive ablation study across ten experimental variants, each averaging validation accuracy over five random seeds to ensure statistical robustness. As a baseline, we use variant_03 (67.8 ± 0.6%), which is identical to our architecture but excludes the central component of our approach.

Among all configurations, variant_08—which integrates the full set of proposed modifications—achieves a validation accuracy of 70.4 ± 0.5%. This represents a substantial absolute improvement of +2.6% over the baseline, with a reduced standard deviation, indicating both higher performance and increased stability. Notably, variant_08 is the only configuration that consistently outperforms the baseline across all seeds, underscoring the effectiveness of our design.

The remaining variants, each omitting or modifying individual aspects of our method, yield validation accuracies in the range of 64.7% to 67.2%, all trailing the baseline. These results emphasize that isolated changes or partial ablations do not account for the observed gains; rather, it is the synergistic combination of our proposed components in variant_08 that delivers significant improvement.

In summary, our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on the validation set, with rigorous ablation confirming that each element of our design contributes to robust gains only when integrated as a whole. These findings highlight both the necessity and the effectiveness of our proposed approach for advancing performance on this challenging task.
